commands: conda run -n ptarcade python3 (derived-mass computation for MAP and benchmarks)
WebSearch dark photon kinetic mixing constraints GeV beam dump SN1987A
WebSearch supercooled dark phase transition reheating BBN N_eff PTA
WebFetch arXiv:2603.08430 (Caputo & Essig, Dark Photon 2026 review)
warnings: The FOPT and PTA analyses fixed epsilon=0 (kinetic mixing) and lambda_portal=0. With epsilon strictly 0 the dark sector is decoupled from the SM and CANNOT reheat the SM plasma: T_reh would be a dark-sector-only temperature and the model would fail BBN as a pure dark-radiation scenario. A nonzero kinetic mixing epsilon ~ 1e-6 to 1e-3 is physically REQUIRED. This portal coupling is the dominant source of new constraints and was not propagated from upstream.

Most decisive constraints (dark photon visible-decay limits, SN1987A, BBN reheating, Delta N_eff, DM relic abundance) depend on epsilon and on the decay widths/branching ratios of Z' and scalon, none of which are computed in any upstream handoff. They require a new portal/thermal-history backend module before any point can be declared excluded or allowed.
approximations: Derived masses computed at face value: m_Zp=g_D*w, m_chi=y*w/sqrt(2), m_scalon=sqrt(beta_lambda)*w.

Reheating-temperature BBN comparison uses T_reh values from handoff_fopt/handoff_pta as if dumped to the SM; validity requires the portal to thermalize before BBN, which is itself an unverified condition.
Dark-photon epsilon allowed-window assessment taken from published compilations (Caputo-Essig 2026, Balan et al. 2025) at the relevant masses, not recomputed.

notes: 1sigma posterior from ptarcade_bayes embedded in handoff_pta.json. Mass ranges quoted over the union of the 3sigma posterior and the four best benchmark points to be conservative.
y=0 is allowed at low end (rank1): then m_chi=0 and chi is massless dark radiation, which sharpens Delta N_eff constraints.

constraints.name: BBN reheating temperature lower bound (SM must reheat above ~few MeV)
constraints.description: After the supercooled transition the vacuum energy reheats the plasma. For successful BBN and neutrino re-thermalization the temperature of the SM bath at the onset of BBN must exceed ~3-4 MeV. Since the dark sector holds essentially all the latent heat (epsilon=0 in the analysis), the latent heat must be transferred to the SM via the kinetic-mixing portal before BBN; otherwise T_reh is dark-only and BBN fails.
constraints.observable: T_reh of the SM bath after percolation + entropy transfer
required_inputs: Z' decay width to SM via epsilon
required_observables: SM reheating temperature after portal energy transfer
missing_observables: fraction of latent heat transferred to SM
Z' lifetime vs Hubble at percolation

conventions.epsilon: L = (epsilon/2) F_munu F'^munu (= kappa in arXiv:2502.19478)

constraints.applicability: Applies to the entire PTA-preferred region; it is the central viability condition.
applicability_notes: The reference model arXiv:2502.19478 finds T_reh = 16.8-55.9 MeV when the portal dumps the latent heat to the SM with kappa ~ 2e-6 to 3e-4, satisfying T_reh>3 MeV. Our dark T_reh down to 3.7 MeV is marginal; the actual SM T_reh depends on the (uncomputed) energy-transfer fraction.
MAP (T_reh~99 MeV dark) is comfortably above the BBN floor IF the portal thermalizes; cannot be declared safe without the transfer calculation.

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: Kinetic-mixing portal required for thermalization with SM
constraints.description: epsilon=0 in the FOPT/PTA analysis makes the dark sector fully secluded. To connect to the SM (thermalize and reheat) a kinetic mixing epsilon is mandatory. arXiv:2502.19478 finds 1e-7 < kappa < 1e-3 gives a common dark-SM temperature before DM freeze-out. This portal then triggers all dark-photon experimental constraints (C4-C6).
constraints.observable: Gamma(Zp <-> SM) / H; dark-SM thermalization condition
required_inputs: SM degrees of freedom at T~m_Zp
constraints.applicability: Entire region; epsilon is a hidden free parameter not yet in the model scan.
applicability_notes: Choosing epsilon to satisfy thermalization+reheating typically lands in 1e-6 to 1e-4, the same window probed by beam-dump/visible-decay searches; consistency must be checked simultaneously.

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: Delta N_eff from the dark sector (dark radiation)
constraints.description: Residual dark-sector species (massless chi when y=0, light scalon, and the dark photon if not fully decayed) contribute dark radiation. CMB/BBN require N_eff = 2.99 +/- 0.17 (Planck), i.e. Delta N_eff <~ 0.3-0.4. If energy is transferred to the SM before neutrino decoupling, Delta N_eff is reduced; the residual depends on dark-to-SM temperature ratio and decay timing.
required_inputs: decay times of Zp and scalon
required_observables: Delta N_eff from dark sector at CMB/BBN
constraints.applicability: Most dangerous for the y->0 subregion (massless chi as dark radiation).
applicability_notes: rank1 benchmark has y=0 exactly -> chi is exactly massless -> permanent free-streaming dark radiation, strongly constrained unless chi is heated only feebly or its abundance is diluted.
Reference uses AlterBBN with initial N_eff=3.044 to evaluate this.

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: Dark photon visible-decay collider/fixed-target limits (BaBar, KLOE, LHCb, NA48, NA64)
constraints.description: For m_Zp ~ 0.3-2.2 GeV with epsilon in 1e-4 to 1e-2, Z' decays visibly to SM fermion pairs (e+e-, mu+mu-, hadrons) and is constrained by e+e- and meson-decay searches. These exclude large epsilon; the surviving window is roughly epsilon <~ few x 1e-4 down to the beam-dump/SN floors.
constraints.observable: sigma(e+e- -> gamma Zp -> gamma ll) and meson decay BRs
required_observables: Z' total and partial decay widths, BR to ll/hadrons

conventions.epsilon: L=(epsilon/2)F F'; visible dark photon limits as in PDG/DarkCast

constraints.applicability: Applies once epsilon is fixed; rules out the high-epsilon part of the required window, not the whole region.
applicability_notes: Cannot be applied from mass alone; production and decay depend on epsilon and on whether Zp decays to chi (invisible) when m_Zp>2 m_chi.

constraints.name: Beam-dump / fixed-target limits (E137, E141, CHARM, NA64, nu-Cal)
constraints.description: Long-lived visibly-decaying dark photons in the 0.3-2.2 GeV range with small epsilon are constrained by proton/electron beam-dump experiments that probe the low-epsilon, long-lifetime regime (roughly epsilon ~ 1e-8 to 1e-5 depending on mass). E137 reaches epsilon ~ 3e-8.
constraints.observable: displaced Z'->ll yield in beam dump
constraints.applicability: Constrains the low-epsilon visible-decay tail; leaves an intermediate window epsilon ~ 1e-6 to 1e-4 partly open at these masses.
applicability_notes: If m_Zp > 2 m_chi (e.g. b3, b4 where m_chi up to 1.7 GeV but m_chi<m_Zp not guaranteed), invisible decay to chi-pairs changes the limit drastically; channel must be determined per point.

constraints.name: Supernova SN1987A cooling bound on dark photon
constraints.description: Dark photons with m_Zp <~ few hundred MeV and very small epsilon (~1e-10 to 1e-6) would be produced in the SN1987A core and shorten the neutrino burst. This excludes a band of low epsilon for the lighter part of the region (m_Zp ~ 0.3-0.5 GeV).
constraints.observable: SN1987A neutrino-burst duration / energy loss rate
required_observables: dark-photon emission rate from SN core
constraints.applicability: Only the lighter sub-GeV part; weakens rapidly above ~0.5 GeV and for epsilon above the trapping ceiling.
applicability_notes: SN band sits below the reheating-required epsilon (~1e-6 to 1e-4) for the bulk of the region; the reheating window is mostly above the SN free-streaming exclusion. Overlap must be checked at the light end.

source.title: Revisiting Supernova 1987A Constraints on Dark Photons

constraints.name: Dark fermion chi relic abundance / overclosure
constraints.description: chi is stable (U(1)_chi number). If it is a thermal relic its abundance must not overclose: Omega_chi h^2 <= 0.12. For m_chi ~ 0.1-1.7 GeV annihilation chi chi -> Zp Zp (secluded) or forbidden/coannihilation channels set the abundance. Underabundance is allowed (then not all DM).
constraints.applicability: Whole region with y>0; for y=0 m_chi=0 and chi is dark radiation (see C3), not DM.
applicability_notes: Reference implements log2(Omega h^2/0.12)=0+/-1 as a likelihood; viable points exist for sub-GeV chi via forbidden/secluded annihilation.

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: Dark matter direct detection (epsilon-mediated chi-nucleus scattering)
constraints.description: If chi is (part of) DM and Zp mixes with the photon, chi scatters off nuclei/electrons with cross section ~ epsilon^2 g_D^2. Sub-GeV DM direct-detection (electron-recoil: XENON, SENSEI, DAMIC) constrains the combination for the fraction of DM in chi.
constraints.applicability: Only if chi is a sizable DM fraction; suppressed if underabundant.
applicability_notes: Sub-GeV nuclear-recoil sensitivity is weak; electron-recoil more relevant; depends strongly on f_chi.

constraints.name: Light scalon decay / cosmological lifetime (BBN injection)
constraints.description: The pseudo-dilaton scalon (m ~ 20-110 MeV) couples to SM only via loops/portal and may be long-lived. A late-decaying or stable light scalar can spoil BBN by electromagnetic/hadronic energy injection or act as extra dark radiation. Its lifetime and abundance must be checked.
constraints.observable: scalon lifetime and abundance vs BBN/CMB injection bounds
required_inputs: scalon-SM coupling (loop via epsilon or Higgs portal)
constraints.applicability: Whole region; scalon is the lightest dark scalar and a generic BBN/Neff risk.
applicability_notes: If lambda_portal=0 strictly, scalon decays only via the two-dark-photon -> SM loop (arXiv:2502.19478) and can be long-lived; lifetime must be computed before applying any BBN injection bound.

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: Perturbativity / triviality of g_D and y; vacuum stability (beta_lambda>0)
constraints.description: Couplings must remain perturbative and the CW vacuum stable. The model enforces beta_lambda>0 via y^4 < 1.5 g_D^4. In the PTA region g_D ~ 0.5-0.8 and y <~ 0.85 are well below 4pi and below the Landau-pole/triviality scale at GeV energies. Directly checkable from handoff parameters.
constraints.applicability: Satisfied throughout the PTA-preferred region (couplings O(1), constraint y^4<1.5 g_D^4 enforced per point).
applicability_notes: MAP (g_D=0.53,y=0.29) and all four benchmarks satisfy y^4<1.5 g_D^4 and remain far from 4pi.

constraints.name: Percolation / completion of the supercooled transition (no eternal inflation)
constraints.description: Strongly supercooled transitions (alpha up to 1e16) risk failing to percolate or entering eternal inflation. The FOPT backend reports percolation_checked and eternal_inflation_checked = true, so completion is verified upstream for the scanned region.
constraints.applicability: Verified for the viable scanned points by the FOPT backend.
applicability_notes: Already enforced upstream; flagged here for completeness.

constraints.name: Higgs invisible/exotic decay and electroweak precision (Higgs portal)
constraints.description: If a Higgs portal lambda_portal were switched on, scalon mixing with the SM Higgs would induce invisible/exotic Higgs decays and EW precision shifts. Since lambda_portal=0 in the analysis and the dark scalar is GeV-scale, these bounds are presently not applicable, but become relevant if a Higgs portal is added for reheating instead of (or with) kinetic mixing.
constraints.applicability: Not applicable with lambda_portal=0; listed in case the portal choice changes.
applicability_notes: Only the kinetic-mixing portal is used; Higgs portal explicitly set to zero.

missing_calculations.observable: Kinetic-mixing portal energy transfer / SM reheating temperature
missing_calculations.suggested_backend_module: portal_reheating.py: solve dark->SM energy transfer through Z' decay/scattering, output T_reh^SM and check >3 MeV
validation_checks: reproduce Balan et al. T_reh=16.8-55.9 MeV for their kappa

missing_calculations.observable: Z' decay widths, branching ratios and lifetime as function of (m_Zp, epsilon, m_chi)
missing_calculations.suggested_backend_module: darkphoton_decays.py (DarkCast-style): Gamma(Z'->ll,hadrons,chi chi), lifetime, visible/invisible BR
validation_checks: compare to PDG dark-photon visible BR

missing_calculations.observable: Delta N_eff from dark sector (xi=T_dark/T_SM, residual chi/scalon/Z' radiation)
missing_calculations.suggested_backend_module: deltaNeff.py (or AlterBBN interface): track dark entropy, output Delta N_eff at BBN/CMB

missing_calculations.observable: chi thermal relic abundance Omega_chi h^2
missing_calculations.suggested_backend_module: relic_chi.py: Boltzmann freeze-out for chi chi->Zp Zp / forbidden channels

missing_calculations.observable: Scalon decay width / lifetime and abundance
missing_calculations.suggested_backend_module: scalon_decay.py: two-dark-photon loop width to SM, lifetime, BBN injection check
